key_insight: Hydrogen binding to a bcc iron screw dislocation depends strongly on core structure, site type, and concentration, and a thermodynamic treatment can collapse this high-dimensional landscape into a small set of statistically relevant trapping states.
engineering_relevance: The results provide a multiscale, physically grounded model for predicting hydrogen solubility and dislocation-assisted embrittlement in iron, useful for larger-scale plasticity and failure simulations.
method: Molecular simulations with a neural-network interatomic potential
Systematic scan of hydrogen binding across easy-core and hard-core dislocation configurations
Thermodynamic ranking of trapping states plus validation of an elastic dipole approximation
evidence_signal: The framework is benchmarked against the limited available DFT data and shown to reproduce experimental hydrogen solubility trends through the easy-core contribution.
watch_item: The elastic dipole description is only valid within a limited interaction regime, so using it outside its domain may miss core-specific and concentration-dependent effects.
paper_title: Hydrogen segregation around a straight screw dislocation in bcc iron
